working_on: Author .github/dependabot.yml + ADR 0069 to adopt Dependabot for pip + github-actions ecosystems; closes Issue #67 and completes SWE-hardening Tier 1.
outcome_summary: SWE-hardening Tier 1 closed at S-0133 — five deliverables landed: .github/dependabot.yml (new, v2 config: pip + github-actions ecosystems, weekly Monday cadence, grouped minor/patch per ecosystem, max 10 open PRs, auto-rebase, Conventional Commits via commit-message.prefix=chore + include=scope); engine/adr/0069-dependabot-pip-and-actions-ecosystems.md (Accepted, ~160 lines, mirrors ADR 0067/0068 shape but lighter — no validator check, no new cross-cutting mechanism; trigger criterion evaluation per ADR 0053 = ZERO criteria satisfied → no first-exercise readiness note required); .github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md gains lockfile-regen reminder line applying to human-authored PRs AND Dependabot PRs (reviewer regenerates before merge); engine/operations/dependency-discipline.md Security-update workflow section rewritten past-tense citing ADR 0069 + correction (Dependabot does NOT regenerate uv.lock — pip ecosystem reads pyproject.toml floors only, reviewer responsibility) + interaction-with-ADRs row Issue#67-pending→ADR-0069-closes-#67; engine/adr/README.md row added + counts 33→34 engine + 70→71 full ADR collection. Closes Issue #67 — last open Tier 1 SWE-hardening item; Tier 1 COMPLETE after this lands. Posture clarifications loaded into ADR 0069 (load-bearing against future confusion): (A) Dependabot PRs are NOT routine-mode commits — originate from GitHub external apparatus, not auto_target.json tasks, routine-mode scope_lock does not apply to a Dependabot PR's diff; (B) 'intentional-only no drive-by refreshes' governs human sessions, not Dependabot's cadence — Dependabot IS the intentional refresh procedure externalized to a weekly schedule; (C) CI (.github/workflows/validate.yml per ADR 0065 engine) is the gate for Dependabot PRs since the pre-commit hook lives in local clones and Dependabot's commits originate server-side; (D) major-version bumps require ADR or amendment if upstream contract changes affect the citing decision. Cost named explicitly: Dependabot does NOT regenerate uv.lock; PR template lockfile-regen reminder + CI hard-fail on `uv lock --check` (per ADR 0065 engine decision 3) are the backstops; future auto-rebase GitHub Action that regenerates uv.lock on Dependabot PR branches would close the gap mechanically but deferred at single-maintainer scale. MemPalace decision drawer filed in paideia/decisions room (id drawer_paideia_decisions_7c224ba73ed8f9ef34699402; per decision drawer captures 5 coupled decisions + 4 posture clarifications + considered-and-rejected alternatives including pip-audit/safety as additional pre-commit gate, auto-merge configuration, invented tier:1/dependencies labels in config, renovate over Dependabot, uv ecosystem if exists, and auto-rebase Action for uv.lock regen). Three commits this session: eager-claim (ef7ac81) + deliverable batch (3a3718a) + closeout (pending). Push pattern: eager-claim via routine_lifecycle_push.py eager-claim mode (shape-verified accepts build-session commits with the same chore(session): eager-claim convention); closeout via routine_lifecycle_push.py close mode at session-end. Deliverable mode wrapper refused this build session's deliverable commit because it requires a task_id (routine-only field); the wrapper correctly discriminates between build and routine lifecycle commits, so deliverable was held local-only until the close push bundles it. Soft-warns at shutdown: issue_collision 26 stable (+2 from S-0132's 24 due to commit-message keyword overlap with #91 + #71; informational only — neither Issue is in scope this session), missing_rigor_score 360 stable, orphan_leaf 1 stable, mempalace_zero_citations_after_search 1 (new fire — boot-search ran 2 calls but pre-final-check the outcome_summary was null; this outcome_summary cites the new drawer ID drawer_paideia_decisions_7c224ba73ed8f9ef34699402 + ADR 0069 + the per decision drawer formulation, so re-run should clear). No new escalation surface. First-PR-arrival empirical verification deferred — wait one week to Monday 2026-05-18; recorded in a follow-up commit to ADR 0069's Empirical record subsection or in next session's outcome_summary, mirroring the ADR 0067 gitleaks T1-A/B/C closure pattern. Next session S-0134 picks from remaining SWE-hardening Tier 2: #71 pytest-cov (needs first-exercise note — cross-cutting), Pairing C #73 + #74 code-review skills, #72 issue templates, or #75 frontend-ui-engineering priority:urgent skill.
